steps.title: Set up the difference and factor
steps.content:
The time difference is $29 \times 287 - 29 \times 252$. Factor out 29:
$$29(287 - 252)$$
Calculate the difference in orbital periods:
$$287 - 252 = 35$$

steps.title: Multiply by the number of orbits
steps.content:
Compute the total difference:
$$29 \times 35 = 1015$$
The second moon takes 1015 more days to orbit the planet 29 times.
